title: Tenant Configuration Audit
spl:
index=cisco_aci sourcetype="cisco:aci:audit"
| where like(affected, "uni/tn-%")
| rex field=affected "uni/tn-(?<tenant>[^/]+)"
| stats count by _time, user, action, tenant, affected, descr
| sort -_time
| table _time, user, action, tenant, affected, descr
description: Configuration changes in ACI tenants (BDs, EPGs, contracts) are a leading cause of outages. Auditing all changes provides accountability, supports compliance, and enables rapid rollback identification when issues occur.
value: Configuration changes in ACI tenants (BDs, EPGs, contracts) are a leading cause of outages. Auditing all changes provides accountability, supports compliance, and enables rapid rollback identification when issues occur.
implementation: Enable audit logging on APIC (enabled by default). Ingest audit records via API polling or syslog. Track all create/modify/delete operations on tenant objects. Correlate configuration changes with fault events. Require change management tickets for production tenant changes.
visualization: Table (recent changes), Timeline (change events), Bar chart (changes by user), Pie chart (changes by tenant).
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: `TA_cisco-ACI`, APIC audit log.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: APIC audit log (`/api/node/class/aaaModLR.json`).
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Enable audit logging on APIC (enabled by default). Ingest audit records via API polling or syslog. Track all create/modify/delete operations on tenant objects. Correlate configuration changes with fault events. Require change management tickets for production tenant changes.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=cisco_aci sourcetype="cisco:aci:audit"
| where like(affected, "uni/tn-%")
| rex field=affected "uni/tn-(?<tenant>[^/]+)"
| stats count by _time, user, action, tenant, affected, descr
| sort -_time
| table _time, user, action, tenant, affected, descr
```

Understanding this SPL

**Tenant Configuration Audit** — Configuration changes in ACI tenants (BDs, EPGs, contracts) are a leading cause of outages. Auditing all changes provides accountability, supports compliance, and enables rapid rollback identification when issues occur.

Documented **Data sources**: APIC audit log (`/api/node/class/aaaModLR.json`). **App/TA** (typical add-on context): `TA_cisco-ACI`, APIC audit log. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: cisco_aci; **sourcetype**: cisco:aci:audit. If that sourcetype is not mentioned in Data sources, double-check parsing or update the documentation to match the feed you actually ingest.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=cisco_aci, sourcetype="cisco:aci:audit". Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• Filters the current rows with `where like(affected, "uni/tn-%")` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
• Extracts fields with `rex` (regular expression).
• `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by _time, user, action, tenant, affected, descr** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.
• Pipeline stage (see **Tenant Configuration Audit**): table _time, user, action, tenant, affected, descr


Optional CIM / accelerated variant (same use case, normalized fields via Common Information Model):

```spl
| tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Change.All_Changes by All_Changes.action, All_Changes.object, All_Changes.user | sort - count
```

Understanding this CIM / accelerated SPL

**Tenant Configuration Audit** — Configuration changes in ACI tenants (BDs, EPGs, contracts) are a leading cause of outages.

This **CIM or accelerated** block uses normalized field names and/or `tstats` over data models. Enable **acceleration** on the referenced models (and correct CIM knowledge objects) or the search may return nothing.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Uses `tstats` against accelerated summaries for data model `Change.All_Changes` — enable acceleration for that model.
• Orders rows with `sort` — combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.

Enable Data Model Acceleration (and metric indexes for `mstats`) for the models or datasets referenced above; otherwise `tstats`/`mstats` may return no results from summaries.



Step 3 — Validate
Confirm in APIC (Admin > Audit Log) and the managed-object work pane; match user, time, and affected DN to Splunk.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (recent changes), Timeline (change events), Bar chart (changes by user), Pie chart (changes by tenant).
grandmaExplanation: Configuration changes in ACI tenants (BDs, EPGs, contracts) are a leading cause of outages — so traffic problems, routing mistakes, and risky changes surface fast.
hardware: Cisco APIC, Nexus 9332C (ACI), Nexus 93180YC-FX (ACI), Nexus 9364C (ACI), Nexus 9504 (ACI), Nexus 9508 (ACI)
cimSpl: | tstats summariesonly=t count from datamodel=Change.All_Changes by All_Changes.action, All_Changes.object, All_Changes.user | sort - count
knownFalsePositives: Tenant changes during scheduled migrations, app onboarding, or controlled fabric rollouts.
